g.text: Outbox Pattern: One Local Transaction Writes BOTH the Row and the Event
The broker is not part of the DB, so you cannot commit a row and a Kafka publish atomically. The outbox
trick: insert the event into an outbox table inside the SAME local txn, then let an async relay drain it.
BEGIN ▾ single local DB transaction ▾ COMMIT
Polling: ~100 ms latency, periodic DB load. CDC (e.g. Debezium): ~ms latency, reads the WAL.
Without outbox: app calls DB AND broker as separate steps.
One can succeed while the other crashes — drift is silent.
Result if the broker call crashes:
• DB has the order; downstream never hears about it.
• Replaying the request creates a duplicate row.
• No retry strategy fixes this without idempotency keys
Outbox eliminates this gap by making the broker irrelevant
to atomicity — the event is just another row in the same DB.
